_comment: Calenbella chunk 12 — Public booking page UI. Agent shipped the page correctly per the chunk's done-when, but the reviewer noticed a missing constraint: when any of the host's calendar connections is in 'degraded' state, the page must show a stale-data warning. This is a Constitution #8 surface (no silent failures affecting the user) that the spec hadn't explicitly named. Verdict: amend_spec (additive — adds a constraint, doesn't relax one).
doneWhen: booking page renders correctly with slots in invitee TZ, hides slots that conflict with host's busy time, submits to a server action.
agentReport:
STATUS: done
DONE_WHEN: met
CHANGED: app/[host-slug]/[event-type-slug]/page.tsx, app/[host-slug]/[event-type-slug]/SlotPicker.tsx, app/[host-slug]/[event-type-slug]/BookingForm.tsx, lib/scheduling/render-slots.ts
TESTS: 18/18
NEW_FAILURES: none
PRE_EXISTING_FAILURES: none
SPEC_GAPS: none
LAUNCH_READINESS: none
NOTE: Booking page renders correctly. TZ detection + override works, slot picker hides conflicts, form submits to server action stub. No issues to flag.

specDiff.additions: **Stale-data warning (degraded connections):** if any of the host's `read_for_availability` calendar connections is in `degraded` state at the time the booking page renders, the page must display a small but visible notice on the slot list — e.g. "Showing the host's last-synced availability — recent calendar changes may not be reflected". Silently rendering stale availability without a warning is forbidden. The page must not 500 in this case; it shows the cached/last-synced view with the notice. (This is the user-facing surface of Constitution #8 applied to a sync-failure mode.)
